title: The Creative Mind: Myths and Mechanisms (2nd ed.)
key_thesis: Foundational work that defines the three-fold taxonomy of creativity — combinational, exploratory, and transformational — and argues creativity is intelligible as a cognitive-computational process amenable to AI modeling.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Source text for 'combinational creativity' as a formal category. Boden's vocabulary lets you specify exactly which mode you're operating in. For an LLM combining business concepts, distinguishes mere recombination (combinational) from search within a structured space (exploratory) from changing the space itself (transformational).
memorable_quote: Creativity is the ability to come up with ideas or artefacts that are new, surprising, and valuable.

title: Creativity in a Nutshell
key_thesis: Compressed, accessible restatement of Boden's three-fold taxonomy with the question of whether computers can be creative as the framing device.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Cleanest published statement of the combinational/exploratory/transformational split. Combinational creativity is defined as producing unfamiliar but interesting combinations of familiar ideas — exactly what an LLM concept-combiner does. Use this as the citation when defining your system's mode.
memorable_quote: Combinational creativity produces unfamiliar combinations of familiar ideas.

title: Computer Models of Creativity
key_thesis: Surveys how AI systems implement each of the three creativity types, arguing combinational creativity is hardest to model because evaluating which combinations are valuable demands rich semantic knowledge.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Explicitly addresses why combinational creativity is computationally harder than it looks — generating combinations is trivial, evaluating them requires deep world knowledge. Central design challenge of an LLM business-idea generator: combination is cheap; valuing the output is expensive.
memorable_quote: Combinational creativity, paradoxically, is the most difficult to model in AI — for it requires a rich store of knowledge, and many ways of moving around within it.

title: Conceptual Integration Networks
key_thesis: Introduces conceptual blending as a fundamental cognitive operation: meaning arises by selectively projecting from two or more input mental spaces into a blended space via a generic space, producing emergent structure absent in either input.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: THE formal model of how two concepts combine to yield something new and not contained in either parent. Four-space architecture (two inputs, generic, blend) is directly applicable to a business-idea generator: input A ('Uber'), input B ('pet care'), generic structure (on-demand service), blend ('Uber for dog walkers') with emergent properties (insurance, GPS, ratings) neither parent dictated.
memorable_quote: Blending is not a compositional algorithmic operation… The blended space typically develops emergent structure not provided by the inputs.

title: The Way We Think: Conceptual Blending and the Mind's Hidden Complexities
key_thesis: Book-length statement of conceptual blending theory; argues double-scope blending — combining incompatible input frames into a coherent blend — is the cognitive innovation that makes humans uniquely creative.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Expands the 1998 paper into a complete theory with hundreds of worked examples. Its taxonomy of integration networks (mirror, single-scope, double-scope) gives a typology for ranking conceptual distance between inputs in a blender — double-scope blends are where the most surprising ideas live.
memorable_quote: The essence of the operation is to construct a partial match between two inputs… and then to project selectively from those inputs into a novel 'blended' mental space, which then dynamically develops emergent structure.

title: Fluid Concepts and Creative Analogies: Computer Models of the Fundamental Mechanisms of Thought
authors: Douglas R. Hofstadter & the Fluid Analogies Research Group
key_thesis: Argues creativity, analogy, and high-level perception are the same cognitive mechanism: fluid, context-sensitive activation of concepts. Presents Copycat, Tabletop, etc.
key_concepts: analogy as the core of cognition
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Hofstadter's 'analogy is the core of cognition' is the precursor to all later combinatorial-creativity work. Copycat shows a creative system needs not just to combine but to flexibly slip between related concepts under contextual pressure. For an LLM business idea generator: 'Airbnb for X' should slip to 'fractional ownership of X' when context demands.
memorable_quote: Making variations on a theme is really the crux of creativity.

title: Some Empirical Criteria for Attributing Creativity to a Computer Program
key_thesis: 18 empirical criteria for judging creativity attribution organized around typicality, quality, and novelty relative to the program's inspiring set.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Standard rubric for evaluating creative AI systems. Forces specifying: (1) what counts as a valid business idea (typicality), (2) what counts as a good one (quality), (3) what counts as novel relative to training/inspiring set. Without this, you can't tell if the system is creating or merely interpolating.
memorable_quote: An item is novel if it is dissimilar to existing examples in some specified inspiring set.

title: A Preliminary Framework for Description, Analysis and Comparison of Creative Systems
key_thesis: Formalizes Boden's framework into the Creative Systems Framework (CSF) using set-theoretic notation: a creative system is a tuple over a universe of concepts with operators for traversal (R), generation (T), and evaluation (E).
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: First rigorous formal specification of what a creative system IS as a computational object. Wiggins gives notation for separating the conceptual space, search/generation rules, and evaluation rules — three distinct modules an LLM idea-generator needs explicitly. Citation for designing the architecture, not just the prompts.
memorable_quote: We have proposed a framework which allows us to describe creative behaviour in computational systems… and to compare different systems on the same basis.

title: Creativity Versus the Perception of Creativity in Computational Systems
venue_or_publisher: AAAI Spring Symposium: Creative Intelligent Systems, SS-08-03, 14-20
key_thesis: Judging a creative system by output alone is wrong; observers must also perceive skill, appreciation, and imagination in the producing process. Introduces the 'creative tripod' — three legs a system must visibly demonstrate.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Counterpoint to pure-output evaluation. For a business-idea LLM, Colton's argument means the system must make its reasoning visible — show that it considered alternatives (imagination), filtered against criteria (appreciation), constructed with technique (skill).
memorable_quote: The way [a system] produces artefacts also needs to be taken into account… it is not appropriate to base the assessment of a system on its output alone.

title: Computational Creativity: The Final Frontier?
venue_or_publisher: Proc. ECAI 2012, Frontiers in AI and Applications, vol. 242, 21-26 (IOS Press)
key_thesis: Field-defining manifesto: working definition ('the philosophy, science and engineering of computational systems which, by taking on particular responsibilities, exhibit behaviours that unbiased observers would deem to be creative'), surveys seminal works, lays out research challenges.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Best paper to cite when establishing 'computational creativity' as a recognized field. Frames the discipline's working definition. Situates LLM ideation work in a 25-year academic tradition with its own evaluation standards.
memorable_quote: Computational Creativity is the philosophy, science and engineering of computational systems which… exhibit behaviours that unbiased observers would deem to be creative.

title: Exploding the Creativity Myth: The Computational Foundations of Linguistic Creativity
key_thesis: Demystifies linguistic creativity by showing it is built from concrete computational mechanisms — analogy, metaphor, blending, neologism, cliché reuse — most of which are recombinative rather than ex nihilo.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Veale's central thesis — creativity is mostly principled recombination — is precisely the philosophical license for an LLM concept-combiner. Provides concrete recipes (Google n-grams as a stereotype source, simile harvesting, slot-filling templates) translatable into LLM prompting.
memorable_quote: Creativity is less about magic than it is about mechanism.

title: Computational Approaches to Storytelling and Creativity
key_thesis: Surveys computational story-generation systems and evaluates them against computational creativity criteria. Narrative generation as a productive testbed for combinational/exploratory creativity.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Shows how to evaluate a generative system against Boden/Ritchie/Wiggins criteria with a concrete domain. Structural parallel to business ideas: both are recombinations of structured elements (character/event/setting vs. customer/value/channel).
memorable_quote: Creativity and storytelling are two ideas that one does not normally find in the realm of computation.

title: Structure-Mapping: A Theoretical Framework for Analogy
key_thesis: Analogical reasoning works by mapping the relational structure (not surface attributes) of a base domain onto a target domain. The systematicity principle: mappings preferring connected systems of relations over isolated predicates produce stronger analogies.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Pre-dates the field but is foundational. When you blend 'Uber' and 'healthcare', you're doing structure mapping: the relational system gets imported, not the surface features. Gentner provides the formal account of what makes a deep blend versus a shallow pun.
memorable_quote: An analogy is an assertion that a relational structure that normally applies in one domain can be applied in another domain.

title: A Standardised Procedure for Evaluating Creative Systems: SPECS
key_thesis: SPECS — a three-step methodology: (1) state what creativity means for the system, (2) derive concrete tests, (3) apply tests. Provides 14 empirically-derived components of creativity.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Defensible evaluation rubric. For an LLM idea generator you need to commit to which of Jordanous's 14 components your system claims to exhibit and how you measure each — without this, evaluation collapses into anecdotal cherry-picking.
memorable_quote: Creativity evaluation has not kept pace with the development of creative systems.

title: Design Prototypes: A Knowledge Representation Schema for Design
key_thesis: Design prototypes as a representation distinguishing routine, innovative, and creative design. Creative design adds new variables; innovative design re-instantiates existing variables in new ranges; routine design fills standard ranges.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Gero's routine/innovative/creative trichotomy parallels Boden's combinational/exploratory/transformational. FBS frame (Function = customer job-to-be-done; Behavior = how the business achieves it; Structure = operational implementation) is directly transplantable as a slot structure for an LLM idea generator.
memorable_quote: Creative design… is concerned with the introduction of new variables that hitherto have not been part of the design representation.

title: The Componential Theory of Creativity
venue_or_publisher: Originally J. Personality & Social Psychology 45, 357-376; 'Creativity in Context' (Westview, 1996); HBS WP #12-096 (2012)
key_thesis: Creative output requires four components: (1) domain-relevant skills, (2) creativity-relevant processes, (3) intrinsic task motivation, (4) social/work environment. Intrinsic motivation principle: people are most creative when motivated by interest, not external pressure.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Bridge from psychology to system design. Four components map onto an LLM idea-generator's architecture: domain knowledge (training/RAG), creativity processes (combinational prompting), 'motivation' (objective function/goal specification), environment (prompt context). Cite Amabile when justifying why a single prompt isn't enough.
memorable_quote: People will be most creative when they feel motivated primarily by the interest, enjoyment, satisfaction, and challenge of the work itself — and not by external pressures.

title: An Investment Theory of Creativity and Its Development
authors: Robert J. Sternberg & Todd I. Lubart
key_thesis: Creative individuals 'buy low and sell high' in ideas: pursue ideas unknown or out of favor with growth potential, persist through resistance, then move on. Six confluent resources: intelligence, knowledge, thinking style, personality, motivation, environment.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Economic metaphor directly useful for a business-idea generator: most valuable ideas are those currently undervalued (low consensus belief) but with high underlying merit. Built-in heuristic for filtering LLM outputs — favor combinations the market hasn't yet recognized over the obvious.
memorable_quote: Creative people are ones who are willing and able to metaphorically buy low and sell high in the realm of ideas.

title: The Standard Definition of Creativity
authors: Mark A. Runco & Garrett J. Jaeger
key_thesis: Traces consensus definition to Stein 1953 / Barron 1955 and crystallizes it: creativity requires both originality (novelty) AND effectiveness (usefulness, fit, appropriateness). Originality alone is bizarreness, not creativity.
relevance_to_combinatorial_creativity: Shortest, most-cited definitional anchor in the field. The dual criterion is the evaluation function: novelty AND fit/usefulness. Drop either and you get gibberish or platitudes. Operational definition of what your system maximizes.
memorable_quote: Creativity requires both originality and effectiveness.
